crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This file is a massive collection of malware signatures and IOCs, appearing to be a database for a security or threat intelligence tool. It contains an overwhelming number of strings referencing a vast array of malware families, APT groups, and ransomware, including but not limited to Lazarus, Cobalt Strike, Conti, Qakbot, Mirai, and WannaCry. The strings are formatted as detection rules from multiple security vendors (e.g., `SIGNATURE_BASE`, `ELASTIC`, `FIREEYE`, `ESET`). The file also contains numerous malicious command templates, such as `vssadmin.exe Delete Shadows` for deleting volume shadow copies and `schtasks /create` for persistence. Although not an executable threat itself, its nature as a comprehensive database of malicious indicators, tools, and TTPs makes it malicious by content and intent.
